w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh wp14

w:r.w:t: By the end of this unit, students should be able to:
performance of plyometric exercise for the upper and lower quarter
Apply progressions and regressions of functional movement patterns to meet individual needs.
Understand basic principles of plyometric training for return to sport decision-making.
force production in a singular, maximum voluntary contraction (MVC)
1-3 sets of 8-12 repetitions with 2-3 mins of rest between sets
sustained, submaximal force production over an extended
Duration: 1-3 sets of >15 repetitions with 1-2 mins of rest between sets
explosive force production in a short period of time
Duration: 1-3 sets of 3-6 repetitions with 2-3 mins of rest between sets
is a form of high velocity training involving a
rapid eccentric contraction quickly followed by a concentric contraction of the same muscle group
stretching of muscle spindle for energy storage and subsequent release
time delay between eccentric pre-stretch and concentric force production
Concentric shortening: resultant power production phase
Blending these three phases constitutes a plyometric
useful in enhancing speed and power of athletic activities like jumping, cutting, pivoting, throwing,
pain, inflammation, acute/subacute soft tissue injury, inadequate foundational
Guidelines for initiating upper extremity plyometrics are not as well-established. Clinicians should utilize a criterion-based algorithm to determine an individual’s readiness

, consisting of: pain-free, functional ROM, no swelling, 20% bilateral comparison of strength and endurance, normal neurological testing, and 20% normative data/bilateral comparison of upper extremity performance tests.
Closed Kinetic Chain Upper Extremity Stability Test
Guidelines for initiating upper extremity plyometrics are not as well-established. Clinicians should utilize a criterion-based algorithm to determine an individual’s readiness

, consisting of: pain-free, functional ROM, no swelling, 20% bilateral comparison of strength and endurance, normal neurological testing, and 20% normative data/bilateral comparison of upper extremity performance tests.
Closed Kinetic Chain Upper Extremity Stability Test
Variations of LE plyometric exercises: jumping, hopping, bounding
Like other mode of exercise, several variables can be manipulated to modify the intensity of a plyometric drill.
A gradual progression of plyometric training is always warranted, especially following injury.
bilateral before unilateral, slow before fast, low before high, short before long,
is quantified by total “foot contacts” and can be determined based on level of athlete experience or exercise intensity.
Signs of form breakdown can also serve as an indicator of fatigue and influence our dosage.
The intensity of UE plyometric drills can be varied
ROM progressions, CKC and OKC progressions, and perturbations. Volume should be carefully monitored to prevent overuse injuries to vulnerable UE structures.
Objective is to proper landing technique for absorption of large impact forces
; often combined with development of explosive power.

wp:docPr.descr:

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 6

A picture containing floor, sport

w:r.w:t: (12-24in height) with feet hip width apart and toes pointed forward
Step off the box and land on the balls of both feet simultaneously
Flex hips, knees, and ankles immediately upon impact.
Landing should be soft and quiet
Hips/knees should not move into adduction + IR/valgus

wp:docPr.name: Picture 7
wp:docPr.descr:
A person doing a handstand

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 7
pic:cNvPr.descr:
A person doing a handstand
Description automatically generated with medium confidence

w:r.w:t: Stand with feet hip width apart and toes pointed forward
, knees, and ankles while driving both arms upward, jumping as high as possible
Land softly by flexing hips, knees, and ankles upon impact.
Avoid taking any steps before the jump
There should be no pause between lowering and extending
The landing should be soft and quiet

wp:docPr.name: Picture 9
wp:docPr.descr:
A person jumping in the air

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 9
pic:cNvPr.descr:
A person jumping in the air
Description automatically generated

w:r.w:t: Stand with feet hip-width apart and toes pointed forward
and immediately extend hips, knees, and ankles
While airborne, bring both knees into the chest
Land softly by flexing hips, knees, and ankles upon impact
Avoid taking any steps before the jump
There should be no pause between lowering and extending
The landing should be soft and quiet

w:r.w:t: Stand with feet hip width apart and toes pointed forward
and immediately extend hips, knees, and ankles while driving both arms upward
While airborne, rotate 180 degrees to face the opposite direction
hips, knees, and ankles upon impact
Avoid taking any steps before the jump
There should be no pause between lowering and extending
The landing should be soft and quiet

w:r.w:t: Stand with feet hip width apart and toes pointed forward
countermovement) while bringing both arms back behind the body
Immediately extend hips, knees, and ankles
while driving both arms up and forward (jumping forward at 45 degrees)
Bring both legs forward to land with both feet ahead of hips
, and then perform a vertical jump for maximum height.
Avoid taking any steps between jumps
There should be no pause between lowering and extending
The landings should be soft and quiet

w:r.w:t: height) with feet hip width apart and toes pointed forward
Step off the box and land on the balls of both feet simultaneously
Flex hips, knees, and ankles immediately upon impact
Rapidly extend hips, knees, and ankles while driving both arms upward, jumping as high as possible.
Avoid pausing between the landing and vertical jump
The landings should be soft and quiet

w:r.w:t: Stand with the left foot on a box (
height) with feet hip width apart and toes pointed forward
Jump up and over the box with the right foot landing on the far side of the top of the box and the left foot landing on the ground
Shuffle up and over the box from side to side continuously for the designated time
The landing should be soft and quiet
Stand to one side of a box (12-14 inch tall) with feet hip-width apart
Jump up onto the box with both feet
Jump off the box, landing on the other side with both feet
Jump up and over the box from side to side continuously for the designated time (
The landings should be soft and quiet

w:r.w:t: Perform a high skip by driving the left knee up, maintain a
angle at the knee, landing on the
Drive arms aggressively in the opposition to the legs
The take off leg should reach full extension at the ankle, knee, and hip for every skip
The goal should be to skip as high as possible, not as far as possible

w:r.w:t: Attach end of harness (elastic) to stationary object or therapist hold
avoids arms crossing over in front of chest
Patient should demonstrate a slight forward lean into the harness throughout the drill

wp:docPr.name: Picture 24
wp:docPr.descr: legs | Get Fit and Fabulous with StaceyT

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 9
pic:cNvPr.descr: legs | Get Fit and Fabulous with StaceyT

w:r.w:t: on your knees, holding the medicine ball with both hands.
Move the ball to your chest and extend through the elbows to throw the ball forwards
Avoid any steps prior to toss
Setup in an athletic base position while holding a medicine ball at your back shoulder with the back elbow high.
Step behind the front foot and drive off the back leg.
As you reacquire your athletic base position, load into the trail hip and rotate your shoulders back.
Explode through the hips as you throw the medicine ball into a wall.
a proper weight shift during the movement. Load into the back hip and then transfer your weight into the front hip as you rotate the hips and shoulders together.
Push hard off the back leg and ensure that you rotate the hips around the front leg as you come through and finish.

wp:docPr.name: Picture 28
wp:docPr.descr: Explore Your Explosive Power with Medicine Ball Workouts

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 19
pic:cNvPr.descr: Explore Your Explosive Power with Medicine Ball Workouts

w:r.w:t: ball with both hands and stand with your feet at shoulder width. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the countermovement by raising the ball above your head and fully extending your body.
Reverse the motion, slamming the ball into the ground directly in front of you as hard as you can.
Receive the ball with both hands on the bounce and repeat the movement.
Avoid hyper lordosis/extension at the lumbar spine as well as hyper flexion upon release.
Set up facing a wall in an athletic base position while holding a medicine ball at waist level with a soft bend in your arms and knees.
Rotate to one side and load back into the hip.
Rotate through explosively with the hips and throw the medicine ball into the wall directly in front of you.
Catch the med ball as it returns from the wall and repeat on the opposite side for the desired number of repetitions.
a proper weight shift during the
movement. Load into the back hip and then transfer your weight into the front hip as you rotate the hips and shoulders together.
Push hard off the back leg and ensure that you rotate the hips around the front leg as you come through and finish.
Kneel with both knees on the floor. Keep your body straight from your knees to your head throughout the exercise.
Fall forward while extending your arms in front of you.
Lower your chest to the floor, as you would with a standard knee pushup.
(option) Push hard off the floor and lift your body back to the starting position. Keep your body straight as you ascend, if possible. Otherwise, bend forward from the waist and walk your hands back

wp:docPr.name: Picture 31
wp:docPr.descr: Med Ball Chest Toss - Exercise How-to - Workout Trainer by Skimble

pic:cNvPr.name: Picture 13
pic:cNvPr.descr: Med Ball Chest Toss - Exercise How-to - Workout Trainer by Skimble

w:r.w:t: Setup in a supine position with your arms extended vertically and a partner standing over your chest holding a medicine ball.
the medicine ball towards you, receive the ball and allow your arms to bend as
As soon as your elbows touch the floor, explode upwards, extend the elbows, and launch the medicine ball vertically

, the patient rapidly drops and catches the ball as quickly as possible, with the ball moving only a few centimeters as it leaves the grasp of the player temporarily before being recaught and drop- ped from the reference position.
Start in a push-up position with hands underneath shoulders, a neutral spine, and feet shoulder-width apart.
Perform a countermovement by lowering chest to the floor, and then explosively push off the floor/wall, allowing the hands to lift off the ground. Clap the hands together in mid-air
Land back in the starting push-up position, and immediately complete the next repetition
